followups.change: Add 'lint' script (minimum: 'tsc --noEmit') before first CI run. package.json does not exist yet — this is a dev deliverable, but without it, the lint job fails on the very first push.

Define coverage.thresholds for the gated modules (classifier, pipelines, security, audit, repl) at 99%. Without this, 'bun run test -- --coverage' does not actually enforce the 99% gate. Must land alongside M1 implementation.

Pin oven-sh/setup-bun@v1 to a SHA rather than a mutable tag. Tag-pinned third-party actions are a supply-chain risk: the tag can be moved to point to a different (malicious) commit. Minimum change: replace @v1 with @v1 + comment citing the current commit SHA for audit traceability. Full fix: SHA-pin all third-party actions (setup-bun, cache, upload-artifact, download-artifact, checkout).

The concurrency group uses github.ref, which for PRs is refs/pull/N/merge. Two PRs from different authors targeting main will land in the same group only if they share the same ref — they won't. This is correct. However, direct pushes to main and PR runs against main use different refs (refs/heads/main vs refs/pull/N/merge), so a main-branch push won't cancel an in-flight PR run. This is probably the intended behavior but should be documented explicitly. No code change required — add a comment clarifying the scoping intent.

The smoke job does not do a git checkout step — it only downloads the binary artifact. This is correct by design but means the smoke runner has no repo context. If a future smoke test needs any file from the repo (e.g., a fixture or config), it would silently fail. Add a comment on the smoke job noting this is intentional and that any future file dependency requires adding a checkout step.
